parameters.text:
=You are analyzing incoming ticket data from a webhook.

INPUT DATA:
subject: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.subject }}
body: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.body }}

OUTPUT REQUIREMENT:
Generate a valid JSON object with exactly these fields:
{
  "category": "{{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body['request-type'] }}",
  "status": "triage",
  "title": "{{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.subject }}",
  "dueDate": "yyyy-mm-dd",
  "description": "string",
  "priority": "low | medium | urgent",
  "source" : "ai",
  "creator" : "external",
  "name": {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.name }},
  "mail": {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.email }},
  "subtasks": see "5. subtasks" below,
  "assignedTo": see "6.assignedTo" below
}

LANGUAGE RULE:
- Detect the language of the input (subject/body)
- If input is in German → write description in German
- If input is in English → write description in English
- Keep field values like priority and status in English (low/medium/high, triage)

FIELD RULES:

1. DEFAULT VALUE:
   - If no information is available → use "No summarize possible! Please contact the creator of the ticket"

2. dueDate:
   - Extract deadline from subject or body if mentioned
   - Recognize keywords: "morgen" (tomorrow), "übermorgen" (day after tomorrow), "nächste Woche" (next week), "bis" (by), "deadline", etc.
   - Calculate actual date based on today's date: {{ $today }}
   - Format: ALWAYS yyyy-mm-dd
   - If "not analysed": today's date + 5 business days (skip weekends)

3. description:
   - Brief neutral summary in 2-3 sentences
   - Focus primarily on body content
   - Keep it factual and concise
   - Write in the same language as the input (German or English)

4. priority:
   - urgent = critical, blocker, system outage, urgent, immediate action required
   - medium = important but not critical, normal priority, standard request
   - low = minor priority, can wait, nice-to-have
   - If you used "No summarize possible! Please contact the creator of the ticket" 
     for other fields → set priority to "low"

5. subtasks

     If the email contains a list of tasks or hints like "Einkaufsliste", "list", 
     "Liste", "Subtask", "Nebenaufgabe", "side-quest" or similar words, extract the 
     individual list items as subtasks.
    - The field subtasks must always be a JSON array of objects.
    - Each object must have exactly this structure:
    - { "name": "short description", "done": false }
    - Rules for each subtask:
      - name must contain a short description of the task.
      - Maximum length: 50 characters.
      - If needed, trim the text but do not change the original meaning.
      - done must always be the boolean false.
      - Do NOT create nested arrays.
      - Do NOT use numeric keys or tuple-like structures.
      - Do NOT return arrays inside arrays.
      - Correct example:
        subtasks: [
          { "name": "Analyze login error", "done": false },
          { "name": "Check server logs", "done": false },
          { "name": "Deploy fix", "done": false }
        ]
      
    - If no subtasks are detected, return: "subtasks": []

6. assigendTo
  You are given a contacts array: {{ $json.assignedTo }}
Each entry has this structure: { "Id": "...", "name": "..." }

Your task:
1. Scan the email text for any person names or name fragments.
2. For each detected name/fragment, find ALL entries in {{ $json.assignedTo }} whose name contains or starts with that fragment.
3. Collect the matched full names (e.g. "Lukas" → "Lukas Becker").

Return ONLY a valid JSON array of full name strings from {{ $json.assignedTo }}.
No explanation. No extra text. Only the JSON array.

Example: ["Lukas Becker", "Tanja Meyer"]
If no matches found: []


CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT:
- Return ONLY pure JSON
- NO markdown code blocks
- NO backticks (`)
- NO additional text, explanations, or commentary
- Response must start with `{` and end with `}`
- Ensure valid JSON syntax (proper quotes, commas, no trailing commas)

parameters.subject: Join Issue Collector: Neue Anfrage [KI-Limit]
parameters.html:
= Von: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.name }}
Email: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.email }}
Type: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body['request-type'] }}
Betreff: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.subject }}
Body: {{ $('Webhook - Incoming Form').item.json.body.body }}

parameters.subject: =Task [{{ $json.body.title }}] has been updated
parameters.text:
=Hello {{ $json.body.name }},

The task-status of your Task [ {{ $json.body.title }} ] has been changed!

The status is now: {{ $json.body.newStatus }}

For more Details have a look at the Board:

https://join-issue.sebastian-buenz.de/html/login.html

Your Join Team

=You are analyzing incoming ticket data from an email.

INPUT DATA:
subject: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.Subject }}
body: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.snippet }}

OUTPUT REQUIREMENT:
Generate a valid JSON object with exactly these fields:
{
  "category": depending on the bodies content either 'technical-task' or 'user-story',
  "status": "triage",
  "title": "{{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.Subject }}",
  "dueDate": "yyyy-mm-dd",
  "description": "string",
  "priority": "low | medium | urgent",
  "name": "{{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.From }}",
  "subtasks" : see "7. subtasks" below,
  "assignedTo" : see "8. assignedTo" below
}

LANGUAGE RULE:
- Detect the language of the input (subject/body)
- If input is in German → write description in German
- If input is in English → write description in English
- Keep field values like priority and status in English (low/medium/high, triage)

FIELD RULES:

1. DEFAULT VALUE:
   - If no information is available → use "not analysed"

2. dueDate:
   - Extract deadline from subject or body if mentioned
   - Recognize keywords: "morgen" (tomorrow), "übermorgen" (day after tomorrow), "nächste Woche" (next week), "bis" (by), "deadline", etc.
   - Calculate actual date based on {{ $today }}
   - Format: ALWAYS yyyy-mm-dd
   - if you find nothing take todays's date + 5 business days (skip weekends)

3. name:
   - analyse the from-field: {{ $('Gmail Trigger').item.json.From }}
   - set a short but meaningful Real Name, according the from field
   - if no realistic name can be set, take the email-address

4. description:
   - Brief neutral summary in 2-3 sentences
   - Focus primarily on body content
   - Keep it factual and concise
   - Write in the same language as the input (German or English)

5. priority:
   - urgent = critical, blocker, system outage, urgent, immediate action required
   - medium = important but not critical, normal priority, standard request
   - low = minor priority, can wait, nice-to-have
   - If you used "not analysed" for other fields → set priority to "low"

6. CAUTION:
   - if the requests topic is not related to a business-use-case just return a             neutral descirption 
   - mark it in the subject as a potential "spam-mail?"

7. subtasks

     If the email contains a list of tasks or hints like "Einkaufsliste", "list", 
     "Liste", "Subtask", "Nebenaufgabe", "side-quest" or similar words, extract the 
     individual list items as subtasks.
    - The field subtasks must always be a JSON array of objects.
    - Each object must have exactly this structure:
    - { "name": "short description", "done": false }
    - Rules for each subtask:
    - name must contain a short description of the task.
    - Maximum length: 50 characters.
    - If needed, trim the text but do not change the original meaning.
    - done must always be the boolean false.
    - Do NOT create nested arrays.
    - DO NOT CREATE, ADD, EDIT or MAP names and Id's from {{ $json.assignedTo }} 
    - Do NOT use numeric keys or tuple-like structures.
    - Do NOT return arrays inside arrays.
    - Correct example:
      subtasks: [
        { "name": "Analyze login error", "done": false },
        { "name": "Check server logs", "done": false },
        { "name": "Deploy fix", "done": false }
      ]
    
    - If no subtasks are detected, return: "subtasks": []

8. assignedTo

  You are given a contacts array: {{ $json.assignedTo }}
Each entry has this structure: { "Id": "...", "name": "..." }

Your task:
1. Scan the email text for any person names or name fragments.
2. For each detected name/fragment, find ALL entries in {{ $json.assignedTo }} whose name contains or starts with that fragment.
3. Collect the matched full names (e.g. "Lukas" → "Lukas Becker").

Return ONLY a valid JSON array of full name strings from {{ $json.assignedTo }}.
No explanation. No extra text. Only the JSON array.

Example: ["Lukas Becker", "Tanja Meyer"]
If no matches found: []


CRITICAL OUTPUT FORMAT:
- Return ONLY pure JSON
- NO markdown code blocks
- NO backticks (`)
- NO additional text, explanations, or commentary
- Response must start with `{` and end with `}`
- Ensure valid JSON syntax (proper quotes, commas, no trailing commas)
